```spthy
theory CP_Signed_Token_Sec75
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  path/2

(*
  Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (Section 7.5)
  One-pass token: A -> B. Origin authentication (B authenticates A) and confidentiality of encData.
*)

let Initiator_A(skA) =
  let pkA = pk(skA) in
  event A_Start(pkA);
  in(pkB);                                 // obtain intended recipient's public key
  new tA;
  new rA;
  new sgnData;
  new encData;
  let ciph  = aenc(encData, pkB) in
  let y     = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, ciph> in
  let sigA  = sign(h(y), skA) in
  let token = <y, sigA> in
  let cp    = <path(pkB, pkA), pkA> in     // models "B → A" certification path carrying A's pk
  event Running('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);
  out(<cp, token>)

let Responder_B(skB) =
  let pkB = pk(skB) in
  event B_Start(pkB);
  in(M1);
  let <cp, token> = M1 in
  let <_cpval, ap> = cp in                   // extract candidate pkA (ap) from certification path pair
  let <y, sigA> = token in
  let <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, ciph> = y in
  let digest = h(y) in
  if verify(sigA, digest, ap) = true then    // hash-then-sign verification
    if bname = pkB then                      // recipient check: intended for this B
      let encData = adec(ciph, skB) in       // decrypt confidential parameters
      event Commit('A', ap, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
      event Secret(encData, ap, pkB)

let KeyReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    event Reveal(pk(sk));
    out(sk)

process:
(
  !(
    new skA;
    let pkA = pk(skA) in
    out(pkA);                      // publish A's public key
    event HonestA(pkA);
    ( !Initiator_A(skA) | !KeyReveal(skA) )
  )
|
  !(
    new skB;
    let pkB = pk(skB) in
    out(pkB);                      // publish B's public key
    event HonestB(pkB);
    ( !Responder_B(skB) | !KeyReveal(skB) )
  )
)

(*********************)
(* Security Lemmas   *)
(*********************)

(* 1) Secrecy of encData, unless a party is compromised *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All v a b i.
     Secret(v, a, b) @ i
     ==>
       (not (Ex j. K(v) @ j))
       | (Ex j1. Reveal(a) @ j1)
       | (Ex j2. Reveal(b) @ j2)
  "

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise *)
lemma injective_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       ( Ex j.
           Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j
           & (All i2. Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i2 ==> i2 = i)
       )
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

(* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise *)
lemma noninj_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

(* 4) Sanity / executability: a complete uncompromised run exists *)
lemma executable_complete_run:
  exists-trace
    "Ex a b t r s p iHA iHB iR iC.
       HonestA(a) @ iHA
       & HonestB(b) @ iHB
       & Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iR
       & Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iC
       & not (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       & not (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
    "

(* 5) Replay-resistance sanity (nonce carried and checked under signature) *)
lemma replay_nonce_bound_to_run:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

end
```

Notes:
- Uses equational built-ins for asymmetric-encryption, signing, and hashing; pairing/tupling is always available by default in Tamarin and needs no built-in.
- All sends/receives use the standard public network channel (single-argument in/out). The reveal helper also listens on the public channel.

theory CP_Signed_Token_Sec75
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  path/2

(*
  Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (Section 7.5)
  One-pass token: A -> B. Origin authentication (B authenticates A) and confidentiality of encData.
*)

let Initiator_A(skA) =
  let pkA = pk(skA) in
  event A_Start(pkA);
  in(pkB);                                 // obtain intended recipient's public key
  new tA;
  new rA;
  new sgnData;
  new encData;
  let ciph  = aenc(encData, pkB) in
  let y     = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, ciph> in
  let sigA  = sign(h(y), skA) in
  let token = <y, sigA> in
  let cp    = <path(pkB, pkA), pkA> in     // models "B → A" certification path carrying A's pk
  event Running('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);
  out(<cp, token>)

let Responder_B(skB) =
  let pkB = pk(skB) in
  event B_Start(pkB);
  in(M1);
  let <cp, token> = M1 in
  let <_cpval, ap> = cp in                   // extract candidate pkA (ap) from certification path pair
  let <y, sigA> = token in
  let <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, ciph> = y in
  let digest = h(y) in
  if verify(sigA, digest, ap) = true then    // hash-then-sign verification
    if bname = pkB then                      // recipient check: intended for this B
      let encData = adec(ciph, skB) in       // decrypt confidential parameters
      event Commit('A', ap, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
      event Secret(encData, ap, pkB)

let KeyReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    event Reveal(pk(sk));
    out(sk)

process:
(
  !(
    new skA;
    let pkA = pk(skA) in
    out(pkA);                      // publish A's public key
    event HonestA(pkA);
    ( !Initiator_A(skA) | !KeyReveal(skA) )
  )
|
  !(
    new skB;
    let pkB = pk(skB) in
    out(pkB);                      // publish B's public key
    event HonestB(pkB);
    ( !Responder_B(skB) | !KeyReveal(skB) )
  )
)

(*********************)
(* Security Lemmas   *)
(*********************)

(* 1) Secrecy of encData, unless a party is compromised *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All v a b i.
     Secret(v, a, b) @ i
     ==>
       (not (Ex j. K(v) @ j))
       | (Ex j1. Reveal(a) @ j1)
       | (Ex j2. Reveal(b) @ j2)
  "

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise *)
lemma injective_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       ( Ex j.
           Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j
           & (All i2. Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i2 ==> i2 = i)
       )
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

(* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise *)
lemma noninj_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

(* 4) Sanity / executability: a complete uncompromised run exists *)
lemma executable_complete_run:
  exists-trace
    "Ex a b t r s p iHA iHB iR iC.
       HonestA(a) @ iHA
       & HonestB(b) @ iHB
       & Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iR
       & Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iC
       & not (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       & not (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
    "

(* 5) Replay-resistance sanity (nonce carried and checked under signature) *)

  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i

```spthy
    event HonestB(pkB);

)

(* Security Lemmas   *)
(*********************)

(* 1) Secrecy of encData, unless a party is compromised *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All v a b i.
     Secret(v, a, b) @ i
```

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

/*

  let <y, sigA> = token in

    if bname = pkB then                      // recipient check: intended for this B
      let encData = adec(ciph, skB) in       // decrypt confidential parameters
      event Commit('A', ap, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
      event Secret(encData, ap, pkB)

  if x = pk(sk) then
    event Reveal(pk(sk));
    out(sk)

    new skA;
    let pkA = pk(skA) in

    let pkB = pk(skB) in
    out(pkB);                      // publish B's public key
    event HonestB(pkB);
    ( !Responder_B(skB) | !KeyReveal(skB) )

(* Security Lemmas   *)
(*********************)

(* 1) Secrecy of encData, unless a party is compromised *)
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All v a b i.
     Secret(v, a, b) @ i
     ==>
       (not (Ex j. K(v) @ j))

(* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise *)

```spthy
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:

/*

  One-pass token: A -> B. Origin authentication (B authenticates A) and confidentiality of encData.

  let y     = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, ciph> in
  let sigA  = sign(h(y), skA) in
  let token = <y, sigA> in
  let cp    = <path(pkB, pkA), pkA> in     // models "B → A" certification path carrying A's pk

  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);

  in(M1);

  let <y, sigA> = token in
  let <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, ciph> = y in

    if bname = pkB then                      // recipient check: intended for this B
      let encData = adec(ciph, skB) in       // decrypt confidential parameters
      event Commit('A', ap, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
      event Secret(encData, ap, pkB)

let KeyReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    event Reveal(pk(sk));
    out(sk)

    !(
      new skA;
      let pkA = pk(skA) in
      out(pkA);                      // publish A's public key
      event HonestA(pkA);
      ( !Initiator_A(skA) | !KeyReveal(skA) )
    )
  |
    !(
      new skB;
      let pkB = pk(skB) in
      out(pkB);                      // publish B's public key
      event HonestB(pkB);
      ( !Responder_B(skB) | !KeyReveal(skB) )
    )
  )

/*********************
 * Security Lemmas   *
 *********************/

/* 1) Secrecy of encData, unless a party is compromised */
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All v a b i.
     Secret(v, a, b) @ i
     ==>
       (not (Ex j. K(v) @ j))

/* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise */
lemma injective_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       ( Ex j.
           Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j
           & (All i2. Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i2 ==> i2 = i)
       )
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

/* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise */
lemma noninj_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

/* 4) Sanity / executability: a complete uncompromised run exists */
lemma executable_complete_run:
  exists-trace
    "Ex a b t r s p iHA iHB iR iC.
       HonestA(a) @ iHA
       & HonestB(b) @ iHB
       & Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iR
       & Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iC
       & not (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       & not (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
    "

/* 5) Replay-resistance sanity (nonce carried and checked under signature) */
lemma replay_nonce_bound_to_run:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

end
```
attempts.code:
theory CP_Signed_Token_Sec75
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  path/2

/* 
  Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (Section 7.5)
  One-pass token: A -> B. Origin authentication (B authenticates A) and confidentiality of encData.
*/

let Initiator_A(skA) =
  let pkA = pk(skA) in
  event A_Start(pkA);
  in(pkB);                                 // obtain intended recipient's public key
  new tA;
  new rA;
  new sgnData;
  new encData;
  let ciph  = aenc(encData, pkB) in
  let y     = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, ciph> in
  let sigA  = sign(h(y), skA) in
  let token = <y, sigA> in
  let cp    = <path(pkB, pkA), pkA> in     // models "B → A" certification path carrying A's pk
  event Running('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event Secret(encData, pkA, pkB);
  out(<cp, token>)

let Responder_B(skB) =
  let pkB = pk(skB) in
  event B_Start(pkB);
  in(M1);
  let <cp, token> = M1 in
  let <cpval, ap> = cp in                   // extract candidate pkA (ap) from certification path pair
  let <y, sigA> = token in
  let <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, ciph> = y in
  let digest = h(y) in
  if verify(sigA, digest, ap) = true then    // hash-then-sign verification
    if bname = pkB then                      // recipient check: intended for this B
      let encData = adec(ciph, skB) in       // decrypt confidential parameters
      event Commit('A', ap, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
      event Secret(encData, ap, pkB)

let KeyReveal(sk) =
  in(x);
  if x = pk(sk) then
    event Reveal(pk(sk));
    out(sk)

process:
  (
    !(
      new skA;
      let pkA = pk(skA) in
      out(pkA);                      // publish A's public key
      event HonestA(pkA);
      ( !Initiator_A(skA) | !KeyReveal(skA) )
    )
  |
    !(
      new skB;
      let pkB = pk(skB) in
      out(pkB);                      // publish B's public key
      event HonestB(pkB);
      ( !Responder_B(skB) | !KeyReveal(skB) )
    )
  )

/*********************
 * Security Lemmas   *
 *********************/

/* 1) Secrecy of encData, unless a party is compromised */
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All v a b i.
     Secret(v, a, b) @ i
     ==>
       (not (Ex j. K(v) @ j))
       | (Ex j1. Reveal(a) @ j1)
       | (Ex j2. Reveal(b) @ j2)
  "

/* 2) Injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise */
lemma injective_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       ( Ex j.
           Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j
           & (All i2. Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i2 ==> i2 = i)
       )
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

/* 3) Non-injective agreement: B authenticates A on the full tuple, unless compromise */
lemma noninj_agreement_B_authenticates_A:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

/* 4) Sanity / executability: a complete uncompromised run exists */
lemma executable_complete_run:
  exists-trace
    "Ex a b t r s p iHA iHB iR iC.
       HonestA(a) @ iHA
       & HonestB(b) @ iHB
       & Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iR
       & Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ iC
       & not (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       & not (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
    "

/* 5) Replay-resistance sanity (nonce carried and checked under signature) */
lemma replay_nonce_bound_to_run:
  "All a b t r s p i.
     Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
     ==>
       (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
       | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
       | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)
  "

end

generated_prompt:
"""
# SAPIC+ Security-Protocol Prompt Framework

Fill in every `⟨…⟩` placeholder and follow every `[Instruction: …]` note to instantiate this framework for a specific security protocol. The completed text is a prompt to be given to an LLM, which should then produce a full Tamarin Prover model of the protocol written in the SAPIC+ process calculus.

---

## 1. Summary

Write a formal security-protocol model in Tamarin Prover using the SAPIC+ process calculus (applied-pi style `let`/`process` blocks, not multiset rewrite rules). The protocol to model is Certification-Path Signed Token Protocol (Section 7.5), in which Initiator A sends a single, one-pass signed-and-encrypted token to Responder B. The goal is that B authenticates A (origin authentication and integrity of included parameters) and that the confidential parameters remain secret to A and B.

## 2. Cryptographic setup

* Use the built-in equational theories for:
  - asymmetric (public-key) encryption
  - digital signatures
  - hashing
  - pairing/tupling
* Declare any additional user-defined function symbols:
  - path/2 to represent an abstract certification path value: path(Bid, Aid). No equations; it is only carried.
* Global modeling assumptions:
  - Public identities are modeled as public keys pk(sk). A and B’s identifiers that appear in messages are their public keys.
  - The “certification path B → A” included in the message is modeled as a pair <path(pkB, pkA), pkA>, i.e., it carries A’s public key alongside an opaque certificate-list term. B “obtains Ap from B → A” by pattern-matching this pair and extracting pkA.
  - Timestamp “current” checks and optional replay-database checks are abstracted; they are represented by carrying tA and rA in the signed structure, but no global time oracle is assumed.

## 3. Roles

Model each protocol role as a separate process definition.

- Role: Initiator (A) — parameters: skA (A’s long-term private key; pkA = pk(skA) is its public identity)
  1. Role-claim event:
     - Emit A_Start(pkA).
  2. Fresh-value generation:
     - tA: a timestamp value (fresh per session).
     - rA: a non-repeating number/nonce (fresh per session).
     - sgnData: a collection of non-confidential parameters (modeled as a fresh value).
     - encData: a collection of confidential parameters (e.g., a secret key or similar) to be delivered to B (fresh per session).
  3. Message construction:
     - First, obtain the intended recipient’s public key pkB from the network (A must know B’s public key to encrypt for B).
     - Construct y = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)>.
     - Compute sigA = sign(h(y), skA).
     - Construct token = <y, sigA>.
     - Construct cp = <path(pkB, pkA), pkA> to model “B → A”.
     - Outgoing message M1 = <cp, token>.
  4. Message reception and checks:
     - None (one-pass sender).
  5. Security-relevant events:
     - Emit Running('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) just before sending M1.
     - Emit Secret(encData, pkA, pkB) indicating A considers encData secret between A and B.
  6. Send/receive actions:
     - Receive pkB from the network (e.g., in(c, pkB)).
     - Send M1 on the public channel (out(c, M1)).

- Role: Responder (B) — parameters: skB (B’s long-term private key; pkB = pk(skB) is its public identity)
  1. Role-claim event:
     - Emit B_Start(pkB).
  2. Fresh-value generation:
     - None required (B verifies and decrypts).
  3. Message reception and checks:
     - Receive M1 = <cp, token> from the network.
     - Pattern-match cp = <_, ap>, extracting ap (candidate pkA).
     - Pattern-match token = <y, sigA>.
     - Pattern-match y = <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, ciph>.
     - Verify the signature: check that verify(sigA, ap) = h(y).
     - Check that bname = pk(skB) (i.e., the message is intended for this B).
     - Decrypt confidential data: encData = adec(ciph, skB).
     - If all checks succeed, accept.
  4. Security-relevant events:
     - Upon successful verification and decryption, emit:
       - Commit('A', ap, 'B', pk(skB), tA, rA, sgnData, encData).
       - Secret(encData, ap, pk(skB)) indicating B considers encData secret between A and B.
  5. Send/receive actions:
     - Receive M1 from the public channel (in(c, M1)).
     - No outgoing messages in this protocol step.

- Key-compromise helper — long-term private-key reveal for compromise-aware lemmas:
  - For any given sk, allow the attacker to request its reveal by providing the corresponding public key.
  - On a matching request, emit Reveal(pk(sk)) and output sk to the attacker.

## 4. Top-level process

Overall system composition (public channel c is the network; rev is the reveal channel):

- Unboundedly many Initiator key holders and role runs:
  - ! new skA; let pkA = pk(skA) in
    - out(c, pkA);  // publish A’s public key
    - event HonestA(pkA);
    - (  // in parallel for this A:
       ! process Initiator_A(skA) as described above
       | ! process KeyReveal(skA): in(rev, x); if x = pk(skA) then event Reveal(pk(skA)); out(rev, skA)
      )
- In parallel, unboundedly many Responder key holders and role runs:
  - ! new skB; let pkB = pk(skB) in
    - out(c, pkB);  // publish B’s public key
    - event HonestB(pkB);
    - (  // in parallel for this B:
       ! process Responder_B(skB) as described above
       | ! process KeyReveal(skB): in(rev, x); if x = pk(skB) then event Reveal(pk(skB)); out(rev, skB)
      )

This composition makes public keys available on the network and allows any A to choose any B (by reading some pkB from the network) without fixed binding between instances.

## 5. Lemmas to include

State each lemma informally but precisely, naming the events it quantifies over.

1. Secrecy of encData:
   - For any occurrence of Secret(encData, pkA, pkB), the attacker never learns encData (no K(encData)), unless pkA or pkB has been revealed:
     - All v pkA pkB i.
       Secret(v, pkA, pkB) @ i
       ==> (not (Ex j. K(v) @ j))
           | (Ex j1. Reveal(pkA) @ j1)
           | (Ex j2. Reveal(pkB) @ j2)

2. Injective agreement (B authenticates A):
   - Whenever B emits Commit('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData), there exists an earlier matching Running('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) by A on the same tuple, and the Commit is injective on that tuple, unless a party was compromised:
     - All a b t r s p i.
       Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
       ==> (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j
            & (All i2. Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i2 ==> i2 = i))
            | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
            | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)

3. Non-injective agreement (B authenticates A, non-injective form):
   - Whenever B emits Commit('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData), there exists a matching Running('A', pkA, 'B', pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData) by A on the same tuple, unless a party was compromised:
     - All a b t r s p i.
       Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
       ==> (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
           | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
           | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)

4. Sanity / executability:
   - There exists a trace with an honest A and B where a complete run occurs (matching Running and Commit) and no key is revealed:
     - exists-trace
       Ex a b t r s p i j.
         HonestA(a) @ _
         & HonestB(b) @ _
         & Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
         & Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j
         & not (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
         & not (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)

5. Optional replay-resistance sanity (nonce carried and checked under signature):
   - If B commits on tuple (..., rA, ...), then rA occurred inside A’s signed Running tuple on the same session view (unless compromise):
     - All a b t r s p i.
       Commit('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ i
       ==> (Ex j. Running('A', a, 'B', b, t, r, s, p) @ j)
           | (Ex k. Reveal(a) @ k)
           | (Ex k2. Reveal(b) @ k2)

## 6. Conclusive sentence

Name the theory something like CP_Signed_Token_Sec75. Use consistent role tags 'A' and 'B' in all agreement tuples, and keep event names, arities, and argument orders identical between the process definitions and the lemmas.
"""
